title: ​The 2026 Red Sox’ run prevention approach has been a disaster out of the gate
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Red Sox started the 2026 season with a 1-5 record, their worst start since 2019.
Boston's pitching rotation, including newly acquired starters, has a collective 5.22 ERA after six games.
The team's defense, which led the league in errors last season, committed six errors in the first six games.

title: Stefon Diggs has trial date set in strangulation, assault case
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Stefon Diggs faces trial on May 4 in Dedham District Court for charges of strangulation and assault.
The charges originate from an alleged assault against Diggs's personal chef during a financial dispute in December.
Diggs and the New England Patriots deny the allegations, citing an unresolved employee-employer financial disagreement.

title: ‘What we had talked about, take that to the grave’: Report reveals details about State Police sergeant who allegedly drove drunk, caused fatal crash
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: State Police Sergeant Scott Quigley was indicted for negligent motor vehicle homicide while intoxicated after a 2023 crash.
The Middlesex DA's office was not notified of the crash, contrary to police reporting requirements for serious accidents.
DA Director Scott Sarsfield was advised not to disclose Quigley's intoxication, which emerged publicly during a trial.

title: Dorchester woman sentenced for fatal stabbing stemming from online feud
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Alyssa Partsch, 33, pleaded guilty to manslaughter for the July 2023 fatal stabbing of Jazreanna Sheppard in Boston.
Partsch received a 15 to 20-year sentence following a plea deal reducing her charge from second-degree murder.
The crime, resulting from an online feud, occurred at the Park Street MBTA station, where Partsch stabbed Sheppard.

title: NBA superstar’s message to Connecticut Sun fans: ‘They’ll be alright, they’ve got UConn’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Connecticut's WNBA team, the Sun, relocates to Houston to become the Houston Comets.
Kevin Durant acknowledges the Connecticut Sun's move but mentions UConn's strong basketball program as a consolation.
Former Celtics owner Steve Pagliuca's bid to relocate the Sun to Boston was blocked by the WNBA.

title: Alex Cora declines to elaborate after scratching Carlos Narvaez hours before Red Sox game
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Carlos Narvaez was scratched from the Red Sox lineup hours before the game against the Houston Astros.
Manager Alex Cora declined to elaborate on the decision, stating it was an internal matter with Narvaez.
Narvaez confirmed the change wasn't due to injury, and the Red Sox lost 6-4 to the Astros.

title: Mike Vrabel says Patriots will pick up Christian Gonzalez’s fifth-year option
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots plan to pick up Christian Gonzalez's fifth-year option, securing him through the 2027 season at $18.1 million.
Gonzalez, a 2023 first-round pick, earned his first Pro Bowl nod and was a second-team All-Pro in 2024.
Coach Mike Vrabel expressed intent for a long-term extension with Gonzalez, noting his improvement and leadership potential.

title: Elias Lindholm, like the Bruins, is getting hot at the ideal time
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Elias Lindholm scored seven points in his last six games, including five goals, as the Bruins continue their winning streak.
Lindholm's performance has surged since moving to a third-line role post-Olympics, winning 62.2% of faceoffs recently.
David Pastrnak leads the Bruins with 66 assists this season, having contributed 23 points in the last 14 games.

title: For this Everett runner, the Boston Marathon has always been personal
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Michelle Davidson, an Everett resident, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Brigham Stepping Strong Foundation.
Davidson's motivation stems from personal impacts of the 2013 Boston Marathon bombings and the city's response.
The Brigham Stepping Strong Foundation focuses on supporting trauma care and honoring first responders.

title: Boston faces budget deficit of nearly $50 million
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston faces a $48.4 million budget deficit, attributed to snow removal, police overtime, and health insurance costs.
Mayor Wu's administration implements hiring delays and spending freezes to address the deficit by the fiscal year's end, June 30.
Councilor Ed Flynn calls for analysis of Boston's reliance on property taxes and creation of a commission to study office vacancies.
